chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q1_analysis: The paper explicitly provides a working link to the reproducibility package (Zenodo) in Reference [31] and mentions it in Section V.A, satisfying the requirement for public availability of artifacts.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q2_analysis: The evaluation relies entirely on standard software simulations using CPU time measurements on Intel Xeon Gold 6209U processors against a benchmark dataset (Internet Topology Zoo). There is no mention of physical testbeds or hardware deployment.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q3_analysis: Section II ('Problem Formulation') contains a clear, explicit formal definition of the fault model. It defines failure scenarios as subsets of edges F where |F| <= k and specifies that nodes only have knowledge of incident failed links (locality constraint).
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q4_analysis: The paper provides a rigorous, data-driven analysis of performance trade-offs in Section V.B. It quantifies the speedup (orders of magnitude) compared to the state-of-the-art baseline across various network topologies and resilience levels (k=2, k=3), effectively measuring the computational cost.

major_weakness: The experimental evaluation is limited to theoretical simulations on standard hardware without any physical testbed deployment or large-scale emulation.
